# Collision Help

> Collision Help is a free educational resource platform for motor vehicle accident victims in the United States. It provides AI-powered damage analysis, insurance claim guidance, settlement and total loss calculators, and 60+ expert guides covering insurance claims, repairs, fault determination, and legal rights. The site also offers optional connections to vetted car accident attorneys nationwide.

Available in English and Spanish. Covers all 50 US states with state-specific insurance laws, fault systems, and statute of limitations information. Sustained through attorney referral partnerships at no cost to users.

## Tools

- [AI Damage Analyzer](https://collisionhelp.org/ai-damage-analyzer): Upload photos of vehicle damage for instant GPT-4o Vision analysis of repair costs, severity, and case strength
- [Settlement Calculator](https://collisionhelp.org/tools/settlement-calculator): Estimate personal injury settlement value based on medical bills, lost wages, pain and suffering, and other factors
- [Total Loss Calculator](https://collisionhelp.org/tools/total-loss-calculator): Determine if your vehicle will be declared a total loss using state-specific thresholds and calculate expected insurance payout

## Guides

- [Car Accident Guides Hub](https://collisionhelp.org/guides): Index of all 60+ expert guides organized by topic
- [Total Loss Dispute Guide](https://collisionhelp.org/guides/total-loss-dispute): How insurance calculates total loss, disputing valuations, salvage options, GAP insurance, and state thresholds
- [Insurance Claims Guide](https://collisionhelp.org/guides/insurance-claims): Filing claims step by step, timelines, dealing with adjusters, denied claims, diminished value, and documentation
- [Repair Rights Guide](https://collisionhelp.org/guides/repair-rights): Choosing your own shop, OEM vs aftermarket parts, supplemental claims, and frame damage
- [Fault Determination Guide](https://collisionhelp.org/guides/fault-determination): At-fault vs no-fault states, comparative negligence, disputing fault, and police reports
- [Rear-End Collision Guide](https://collisionhelp.org/guides/rear-end-collisions): Fault rules, whiplash claims, chain reactions, and settlement expectations
- [Commercial Vehicle Guide](https://collisionhelp.org/guides/commercial-vehicle): Truck accidents, Uber/Lyft claims, delivery truck collisions, and fleet vehicle liability
- [Accident Injuries Guide](https://collisionhelp.org/guides/accident-injuries): Common injuries, delayed symptoms, whiplash treatment, TBI, back injuries, and pain and suffering

## Reference

- [Glossary](https://collisionhelp.org/glossary): 40+ defined terms used in car accident and insurance claims, from ACV and comparative negligence to total loss thresholds and subrogation
- [Resources](https://collisionhelp.org/resources): Free downloadable checklists, templates, and tools for managing accident claims
- [Blog](https://collisionhelp.org/blog): Articles on car accidents, insurance claims, vehicle damage assessment, and legal guidance

## Locations

- [Car Accident Help by State](https://collisionhelp.org/locations): State-specific insurance requirements, fault systems, and local resources for all 50 states
- [Find a Car Accident Lawyer](https://collisionhelp.org/car-accident-lawyer): Directory of car accident attorneys organized by state and city, covering 770+ cities nationwide

## About

- [About Collision Help](https://collisionhelp.org/about): Mission, business model transparency, what the platform offers, values, and trust information
- [Contact](https://collisionhelp.org/about): General inquiries at help@collisionhelp.org, privacy questions at privacy@collisionhelp.org, legal matters at legal@collisionhelp.org

## Optional

- [Privacy Policy](https://collisionhelp.org/privacy): Data collection, usage, sharing practices, security measures, and user rights
- [Terms of Service](https://collisionhelp.org/terms): Service terms, disclaimers, attorney connection details, and liability limitations
- [Sitemap](https://collisionhelp.org/sitemap.xml): Full site structure in XML format
